api/Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.AxiamAmqpConsumer.htm.summary: Class AxiamAmqpConsumer Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Amqp Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll AMQP event consumer: verify-before-handler HMAC gate plus the sdks/CONTRACT.md §8 ack/nack matrix, built on RabbitMQ.Client 7.x's async consumer API with automatic connection/topology recovery enabled (D-11). StartAsync(string, string, byte[], Func<byte[], CancellationToken, Task>, ILogger?, TimeSpan?, CancellationToken) NEVER hands an unverified delivery to the caller-supplied handler — Verify(byte[], byte[]) runs first, and the handler call site is structurally unreachable until it returns true. The remaining outcomes follow the exact §8 decision matrix: HMAC verification fails → BasicNackAsync(requeue: false) + a security-event log entry that never contains the received or expected HMAC value; the handler is never invoked. HMAC verifies but the NEW-4 replay-protection gate (ReplayGuard) rejects the delivery — key_version below MinKeyVersion, a stale/future issued_at, or a replayed nonce — → BasicNackAsync(requeue: false) + a security-event log entry; the handler is never invoked. Handler returns normally → BasicAckAsync. Handler throws PoisonMessageException (poison message) → BasicNackAsync(requeue: false). Handler throws any other exception (transient/retryable) → BasicNackAsync(requeue: true). public sealed class AxiamAmqpConsumer : IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object AxiamAmqpConsumer Implements IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields DefaultNetworkRecoveryInterval Default network-recovery interval applied to the connection factory. public static readonly TimeSpan DefaultNetworkRecoveryInterval Field Value TimeSpan DefaultPrefetchCount Default QoS prefetch count. public const ushort DefaultPrefetchCount = 10 Field Value ushort Methods DisposeAsync() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources asynchronously. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task that represents the asynchronous dispose operation. StartAsync(string, string, byte[], Func<byte[], CancellationToken, Task>, ILogger?, TimeSpan?, CancellationToken) Connects to amqpUri, opens a channel with automatic recovery enabled, and registers an RabbitMQ.Client.Events.AsyncEventingBasicConsumer on queue that verifies every delivery's HMAC-SHA256 signature (§8, via Verify(byte[], byte[])) BEFORE handler is invoked, then applies the §8 ack/nack matrix documented on this class. public Task StartAsync(string amqpUri, string queue, byte[] signingKey, Func<byte[], CancellationToken, Task> handler, ILogger? logger = null, TimeSpan? replaySkew = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters amqpUri string The AMQP connection URI. queue string The queue name to consume from. signingKey byte[] The per-tenant AMQP HMAC signing secret (§8.1 — obtain from the AXIAM management API; never hardcode). handler Func<byte[], CancellationToken, Task> Invoked ONLY after HMAC verification AND the NEW-4 replay-protection gate (ReplayGuard) both succeed. Throw PoisonMessageException to drop a message without requeue; any other exception is treated as transient and requeues. logger ILogger Receives the §8.4 security event on HMAC verification (or NEW-4 replay-protection) failure; the event never contains the HMAC, nonce, or issued_at value. Defaults to a silent no-op logger. replaySkew TimeSpan? The NEW-4 freshness window applied to each delivery's issued_at field (also sets the nonce dedup store's TTL, 2×replaySkew). Defaults to DefaultSkew (±5 minutes) when null or non-positive. cancellationToken CancellationToken Cancellation token for connection/channel setup and for every ack/nack call issued by the registered consumer. Returns Task

api/Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.Hmac.html.title: Class Hmac | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.Hmac.html.summary: Class Hmac Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Amqp Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll HMAC-SHA256 verify-before-handler primitive for inbound AMQP messages (sdks/CONTRACT.md §8, D-11). Canonicalization is wire/insertion-order preserving, NEVER alphabetically sorted. The canonical Rust signer (crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs's sign_payload) signs the serde_json struct-declaration-order serialization of the message body (with hmac_signature absent). JsonObject is backed by an ordered dictionary and preserves insertion order from parsing by default — Remove(string) mutates that same ordered map in place, preserving the relative order of all remaining keys. This is the single load-bearing property of this class: do NOT introduce an alphabetized copy, OrderBy/Sort, or a POCO deserialize-then-reserialize round-trip here — any of those would silently reorder fields and break every cross-language HMAC verification. public static class Hmac Inheritance object Hmac Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Verify(byte[], byte[]) Returns true iff body's hmac_signature field matches HMAC-SHA256(signingKey, canonical_json_of(body_without_hmac_signature)), computed via a constant-time comparison. Never throws: malformed JSON, a missing/null signature, non-hex signature text, or a wrong-length signature all verify as false — matching §8.3's strict-mode default that rejects (rather than silently accepts) an unparseable or absent signature. public static bool Verify(byte[] signingKey, byte[] body) Parameters signingKey byte[] The per-tenant AMQP HMAC signing secret (§8.1). body byte[] The raw AMQP delivery body bytes. Returns bool true if the signature is valid, false otherwise.

Creates a ReplayGuard with the given freshness skew (defaults to DefaultSkew when skew is null or non-positive). public ReplayGuard(TimeSpan? skew = null) Parameters skew TimeSpan? Fields DefaultSkew The default freshness window applied to a delivery's issued_at field (mirrors crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs's DEFAULT_FRESHNESS_SKEW_SECS = 300). public static readonly TimeSpan DefaultSkew Field Value TimeSpan MinKeyVersion The lowest AMQP signed-envelope key_version this SDK will accept. A message with key_version below this predates the mandatory nonce/issued_at replay-protection fields and is rejected outright — a hard cutover, there is no v1 grace path (mirrors crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs's MIN_ACCEPTED_KEY_VERSION). public const int MinKeyVersion = 2 Field Value int Properties Skew The freshness skew this guard was constructed with. public TimeSpan Skew { get; } Property Value TimeSpan Methods Check(ReplayMetadata) Validates metadata against the NEW-4 replay-protection policy and, iff it passes, atomically records its nonce so a second delivery of the same nonce is rejected as a replay. Never throws: a malformed body, or one missing nonce/issued_at entirely, returns false (which Check(ReplayMetadata)'s caller must treat as a rejection — an unparseable/absent replay-protection field is exactly as unsafe as a stale one). A missing/non-numeric key_version yields KeyVersion == 0, which is always rejected by Check(ReplayMetadata) since it is below MinKeyVersion. public static bool TryExtractMetadata(byte[] body, out ReplayMetadata metadata) Parameters body byte[] metadata ReplayMetadata Returns bool

api/Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAuthMiddle.title: Class AxiamAuthMiddleware | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAuthMiddle.summary: Class AxiamAuthMiddleware Namespace Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore Assembly Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.dll ASP.NET Core authentication middleware (D-06, CONTRACT.md §10 "ASP.NET Core" row: app.UseMiddleware<AxiamAuthMiddleware>() in Program.cs). Mirrors sdks/java/.../spring/AxiamAuthenticationFilter.java's authoritative sequence (extract → verify → exp-check → cross-tenant-check → inject-identity → 401 JSON on failure), adapted to ASP.NET Core's ClaimsPrincipal/User integration point — the direct analog of Java D-14's Spring SecurityContext integration. public sealed class AxiamAuthMiddleware Inheritance object AxiamAuthMiddleware Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Security-critical invariants (T-21-17, T-21-19, T-21-20): When NO credential is presented at all, the request is passed through unauthenticated — the framework's own [Authorize]/authorization middleware 401s it (via AxiamAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler, registered by AddAxiamAspNetCore(IServiceCollection, Action<AxiamOptions>)). This middleware never rejects a request for the mere absence of a token (Java filter lines 78-83 precedent) — doing so would incorrectly 401 public/anonymous endpoints that never carry a token at all. When a token IS presented, it is verified via the shared AxiamClient's internal JwksVerifier local fast path: alg-pinned Ed25519 signature check, THEN the mandatory post-signature tenant_id claim check (Pitfall 3 — the JWKS document is organization-wide, not tenant-scoped, so signature validity alone never implies tenant authorization). An explicit exp re-check is performed here too as defense in depth, mirroring the Java filter's lines 88-91 even though JwksVerifier.VerifyAsync already checks exp internally. User is rebuilt from scratch on every request from a fresh VerifyAsync call — it is NEVER cached beyond the current request, and never beyond the token's own remaining TTL (§10). Every failure path writes a standardized JSON body via WriteAsJsonAsync<TValue>(HttpResponse, TValue, CancellationToken) (System.Text.Json) — never manual string concatenation, so a message containing quotes/control characters can never produce malformed or injected JSON (PATTERNS.md "Standardized JSON error body"). CSRF (cookie double-submit, CONTRACT.md §3): when the credential was sourced from the axiam_access COOKIE (not the Authorization header) and the request method is state-changing (anything other than GET/HEAD/OPTIONS), this middleware additionally requires the X-CSRF-Token request header to be present and equal (constant-time, FixedTimeEquals(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)) to the axiam_csrf cookie value, rejecting with 403 on mismatch/absence. Bearer-header requests are CSRF-immune by construction — a cross-site attacker cannot set arbitrary request headers — but a cookie automatically attached by the browser is not, and in any same-site deployment where axiam_access reaches this app, the non-httpOnly axiam_csrf cookie does too. This mirrors, locally, the same double-submit check the AXIAM server performs on its own endpoints (§3). Constructors AxiamAuthMiddleware(RequestDelegate) Constructs the middleware. Registered by the ASP.NET Core pipeline (app.UseMiddleware<AxiamAuthMiddleware>()), which supplies next. public AxiamAuthMiddleware(RequestDelegate next) Parameters next RequestDelegate The next delegate in the middleware pipeline. Methods InvokeAsync(HttpContext, AxiamClient, IOptions<AxiamOptions>) Convention-based ASP.NET Core middleware invocation. client and optionsAccessor are resolved per-request from RequestServices (the standard UseMiddleware<T> pattern for additional InvokeAsync parameters beyond HttpContext) — they are singletons registered by AddAxiam(IServiceCollection, Action<AxiamOptions>). public Task InvokeAsync(HttpContext context, AxiamClient client, IOptions<AxiamOptions> optionsAccessor) Parameters context HttpContext client AxiamClient optionsAccessor IOptions<AxiamOptions> Returns Task

api/Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAuthorizat.title: Class AxiamAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAuthorizat.summary: Class AxiamAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler Namespace Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore Assembly Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.dll Custom IAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler that writes the standardized JSON error body (CONTRACT.md §10/§11, PATTERNS.md "Standardized JSON error body") for every authorization outcome: an unauthenticated result (401), a "lacks permission" result (403 — AxiamPolicyHandler left an AxiamRequirement unsatisfied, i.e. AuthzError), an unresolvable resource id (400 invalid_request, CONTRACT.md §11.2.3), and a transport failure while checking access (503 authz_unavailable, fail-closed per CONTRACT.md §11.2.5). On success, the pipeline continues exactly like the framework default. public sealed class AxiamAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler : IAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler Inheritance object AxiamAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler Implements IAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This handler deliberately does NOT branch on Forbidden/Challenged. Neither this package nor a typical consumer registers an ASP.NET Core authentication scheme (AddAuthentication()/UseAuthentication()) — identity comes entirely from AxiamAuthMiddleware setting User directly. Because of that, Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.Policy.PolicyEvaluator's internal authentication step always resolves to AuthenticateResult.NoResult() (Succeeded == false) since the evaluated policy carries no AuthenticationSchemes — which means EVERY non-success outcome, including an authenticated-but-forbidden one, would surface as Challenged, never Forbidden. Branching on User's own Identity.IsAuthenticated instead sidesteps that ambiguity entirely and is the authoritative signal: AxiamAuthMiddleware is the only code path that ever sets an authenticated identity, and it only does so after JwksVerifier signature+tenant verification succeeds. Methods HandleAsync(RequestDelegate, HttpContext, AuthorizationPolicy, PolicyAuthorizationResult) On success, continues the pipeline exactly like the framework default. On failure, writes a standardized JSON error body, checking Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamPolicyHandler.OutcomeItemKey in context's Items FIRST — see that key's remarks for why this, rather than authorizeResult's own AuthorizationFailure, is the channel AxiamPolicyHandler actually reaches this handler through: 503 (authz_unavailable) for a transport failure while checking access, 400 (invalid_request) for an unresolvable resource id. Absent either signal, falls back to the §10 behavior: 403 (authorization_denied) when User is already authenticated (an AxiamRequirement was left unsatisfied by AxiamPolicyHandler), otherwise 401 (authentication_failed). See the type-level remarks for why this branches on User.Identity.IsAuthenticated rather than authorizeResult's own Forbidden/Challenged distinction. public Task HandleAsync(RequestDelegate next, HttpContext context, AuthorizationPolicy policy, PolicyAuthorizationResult authorizeResult) Parameters next RequestDelegate The next delegate in the pipeline; invoked only on success. context HttpContext The current request's HttpContext. policy AuthorizationPolicy The policy that was evaluated. authorizeResult PolicyAuthorizationResult The outcome of evaluating policy. Returns Task

api/Axiam.Sdk.Auth.JwksVerifier.html.summary: Class JwksVerifier Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Auth Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll Local EdDSA (Ed25519) JWKS verification (D-02, CONTRACT.md §10 local-verification fast path). .NET has no native Ed25519/EdDSA support anywhere in System.Security.Cryptography (confirmed research finding — dotnet/runtime #14741/#63174 remain unimplemented through .NET 10 GA), so this class uses BouncyCastle.Cryptography's Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Signers.Ed25519Signer / Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters.Ed25519PublicKeyParameters directly — the single, well-vetted, verify-only crypto dependency D-02 permits. public sealed class JwksVerifier Inheritance object JwksVerifier Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Security-critical invariants (T-21-06, T-21-07): alg is pinned to "EdDSA" and checked BEFORE any key (kid) lookup — the token's own header is never trusted to select its verifier (alg-confusion defense). AFTER signature verification succeeds, the tenant_id claim is checked against the caller-supplied expected tenant. The JWKS document is organization-wide, not tenant-scoped, so a valid signature alone never implies tenant authorization (Pitfall 3 — independently confirmed by every sibling SDK). VerifyAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) NEVER throws for attacker-controlled input — every failure mode (bad alg, unknown kid, tampered/invalid signature, wrong tenant, expired token, malformed/non-base64/truncated token) returns null. This matches the AMQP HMAC verifier's fail-closed convention (Amqp/Hmac.cs). Constructors JwksVerifier(HttpClient, Uri, TimeSpan) public JwksVerifier(HttpClient httpClient, Uri baseUrl, TimeSpan cacheTtl) Parameters httpClient HttpClient Used only to fetch the JWKS document; ownership stays with the caller. baseUrl Uri The AXIAM server base URL; the JWKS path is resolved relative to it. cacheTtl TimeSpan How long a fetched JWKS document is trusted before a refetch is forced. Methods VerifyAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Verifies jwt's EdDSA signature against the cached (or freshly fetched) org-wide JWKS AND checks the mandatory tenant_id claim against expectedTenantId. Returns the decoded claims payload on success; returns null for ANY failure. Never throws on malformed or attacker-controlled input — see the type-level remarks for the fail-closed contract. public Task<JsonElement?> VerifyAsync(string jwt, string expectedTenantId, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters jwt string expectedTenantId string cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task<JsonElement?>

api/Axiam.Sdk.Auth.RefreshGuard.html.title: Class RefreshGuard | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.Auth.RefreshGuard.html.summary: Class RefreshGuard Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Auth Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll Single-flight token-refresh guard (D-10, CONTRACT.md §9): guarantees exactly one in-flight refresh call at a time, regardless of how many concurrent callers observe an expired/near-expiry access token. One instance is constructed per AxiamClient and shared across the REST and gRPC transports (D-10's "one guard across REST + gRPC on one client" requirement) — never a second instance per transport. public sealed class RefreshGuard : IDisposable Inheritance object RefreshGuard Implements IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Correctness invariants (mirrors the Java sibling's RefreshGuard double-check- after-lock structure, adapted to SemaphoreSlim + Task<TResult> per CONTRACT.md §9's locked C# mechanism): The refresh delegate is deliberately awaited WHILE still holding the gate. This is what makes every concurrent caller queue on _gate.WaitAsync: the first caller to acquire the gate assigns _inFlight and awaits it before releasing; any caller that later acquires the gate finds _inFlight already completed (and, if still fresh, reuses it) instead of starting a redundant refresh (SC#2: 5 concurrent callers against an expired token ⇒ exactly 1 underlying refresh). On failure, _inFlight is cleared before rethrowing so a failed refresh is NEVER cached for the next caller — the next call always starts a fresh refresh attempt (§9.3: no retry loop inside the guard itself; the caller must re-authenticate from scratch). A subsequent call reuses an already-completed result only when it is still fresh (more than Axiam.Sdk.Auth.RefreshGuard.FreshnessMargin from expiry) — never a stale or faulted one. Invoked at most once per in-flight refresh, regardless of how many concurrent callers invoke RefreshIfNeededAsync(CancellationToken) (§9 single-flight guarantee). Methods Dispose() Disposes the underlying SemaphoreSlim gate. public void Dispose() RefreshIfNeededAsync(CancellationToken) Called by both the reactive (401 / gRPC UNAUTHENTICATED) and proactive (near-expiry, JWKS-driven) refresh paths. Guarantees exactly one underlying refresh call regardless of how many callers invoke this concurrently. public Task<TokenPair> RefreshIfNeededAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task<TokenPair>

api/Axiam.Sdk.Core.NetworkError.html.title: Class NetworkError | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.Core.NetworkError.html.summary: Class NetworkError Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Core Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll Transport-level failure: connection refused, timeout, TLS error, DNS failure, or a server-side 5xx (CONTRACT.md §2). public sealed class NetworkError : Exception, ISerializable Inheritance object Exception NetworkError Implements ISerializable Inherited Members Exception.GetBaseException() Exception.GetType() Exception.ToString() Exception.Data Exception.HelpLink Exception.HResult Exception.InnerException Exception.Message Exception.Source Exception.StackTrace Exception.TargetSite object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Redact-before-wrap (D-12, CR-04 carry-forward): this is the ONLY error class that may be constructed from an HTTP response, and there is exactly ONE construction path that accepts a live HttpResponseMessage — the static FromResponse(HttpResponseMessage, string) factory. There is no public or internal constructor that accepts an HttpResponseMessage directly; the live response object is NEVER stored as the InnerException or in Data — only a pre-sanitized message string survives. This structurally prevents the token-leak-via-error class of bug first found in the TypeScript sibling SDK (Phase 17 CR-04, sdks/typescript/src/core/errorMapper.ts sanitizeAxiosError) and mirrored across every later sibling SDK. Methods FromException(Exception, string) Builds a NetworkError from a caught exception (e.g. a socket/TLS/DNS failure). The exception's own Message is defensively regex-sanitized before being folded into the resulting message, in case a lower-level exception echoed a request header verbatim. public static NetworkError FromException(Exception ex, string context) Parameters ex Exception context string Returns NetworkError FromResponse(HttpResponseMessage, string) Builds a NetworkError from a live HttpResponseMessage. This is the ONLY construction path that accepts a live response — every other code path in the SDK MUST go through this factory (or FromException(Exception, string)) rather than constructing a NetworkError directly from a raw response/exception. public static NetworkError FromResponse(HttpResponseMessage response, string context) Parameters response HttpResponseMessage context string Returns NetworkError

api/Axiam.Sdk.Core.Sensitive-1.html.title: Struct Sensitive<T> | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.Core.Sensitive-1.html.summary: Struct Sensitive<T> Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Core Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll Wraps a token-carrying value so it can never be accidentally exposed via ToString(), System.Text.Json serialization, or a public getter (CONTRACT.md §7, D-12). Only SDK-internal code may construct an instance or read the wrapped value, via Axiam.Sdk.Core.Sensitive<T>.Reveal() — there is intentionally no public accessor. [JsonConverter(typeof(SensitiveJsonConverterFactory))] public readonly struct Sensitive<T> : IEquatable<Sensitive<T>> Type Parameters T Implements IEquatable<Sensitive<T>> Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Per CONTRACT.md §7's C# row ("Struct with ToString() override returning "[SENSITIVE]""): ToString() and the paired SensitiveJsonConverter<T> both always emit the literal "[SENSITIVE]", regardless of the wrapped value. Equals(object?), Equals(T) and GetHashCode() are overridden to make a Sensitive<T> never value-comparable: Equals(object?) always returns false and GetHashCode() always returns a constant. This is deliberate — the default ValueType equality a struct would otherwise inherit performs a structural, field-by-field value comparison (via reflection, and, for a string field, a non-constant-time string.Equals), which would open a value-equality/timing side channel on the redacted value and would transitively leak into any compiler-synthesized record equality of a type that carries a Sensitive<T> field. Overriding to a constant closes that channel. Methods Equals(Sensitive<T>) A Sensitive<T> is intentionally never equal to anything — this suppresses the structural value comparison a struct would otherwise inherit from ValueType, which would be a value-equality/timing side channel on the wrapped secret. public bool Equals(Sensitive<T> other) Parameters other Sensitive<T> Returns bool Equals(object?) A Sensitive<T> is intentionally never equal to anything — this suppresses the structural value comparison a struct would otherwise inherit from ValueType, which would be a value-equality/timing side channel on the wrapped secret. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object Returns bool GetHashCode() Always returns a constant — never derives a hash from the wrapped value. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int ToString() Always returns the redacted literal, never the wrapped value. public override string ToString() Returns string Operators operator ==(Sensitive<T>, Sensitive<T>) Never equal (see Equals(object?)). public static bool operator ==(Sensitive<T> left, Sensitive<T> right) Parameters left Sensitive<T> right Sensitive<T> Returns bool operator !=(Sensitive<T>, Sensitive<T>) Always unequal (see Equals(object?)). public static bool operator !=(Sensitive<T> left, Sensitive<T> right) Parameters left Sensitive<T> right Sensitive<T> Returns bool

DuplexStreamingServerMethod<TRequest, TResponse>) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The token accessor is a caller-supplied, non-blocking Func<TResult> (mirrors the Java AuthClientInterceptor's Supplier<String> and Go interceptor.go's TokenFunc) — this class NEVER calls RefreshIfNeededAsync(CancellationToken) synchronously on the hot AsyncUnaryCall<TResponse> interception path; the accessor is read once per call to build outgoing metadata, and the guard is only ever awaited (fully asynchronously) after an UNAUTHENTICATED response has already been observed. Sync-safety: every code path in this class is async/await all the way down — there is no .Result/.Wait()/.GetAwaiter().GetResult() anywhere, so this interceptor can never sync-over-async deadlock a caller's synchronization context (there is none here to deadlock on, by construction). Constructors AuthInterceptor(Func<string?>, string, RefreshGuard) public AuthInterceptor(Func<string?> tokenAccessor, string tenantId, RefreshGuard refreshGuard) Parameters tokenAccessor Func<string> A non-blocking accessor for the currently cached access token (null when none is available yet). MUST NOT acquire RefreshGuard's lock or perform I/O — this runs on the hot RPC path (mirrors the REST AxiamHttpMessageHandler and the Java/Go sibling interceptors' token-accessor discipline). tenantId string The client's configured tenant identifier (CONTRACT.md §5), injected as x-tenant-id metadata on every call — mirrors the REST X-Tenant-Id header's use of the raw configured value. refreshGuard RefreshGuard The SAME RefreshGuard instance the client's REST transport uses (D-10's "one guard across REST + gRPC on one client") — never a second instance. Methods AsyncUnaryCall<TRequest, TResponse>(TRequest, ClientInterceptorContext<TRequest, TResponse>, AsyncUnaryCallContinuation<TRequest, TResponse>) Injects auth/tenant metadata on every outgoing unary call (CheckAccess/ BatchCheckAccess are both unary RPCs); on UNAUTHENTICATED, drives exactly one shared-guard refresh then retries the call exactly once (§9.3 — never a loop). api/Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.AxiamGrpcAuthzClient..title: Class AxiamGrpcAuthzClient | AXIAM .NET SDK
api/Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.AxiamGrpcAuthzClient..summary: Class AxiamGrpcAuthzClient Namespace Axiam.Sdk.Grpc Assembly Axiam.Sdk.dll gRPC authorization transport (CONTRACT.md §1/§2/§5/§9, D-10). Wraps ONE long-lived GrpcChannel (via AxiamGrpcChannel — strict TLS, §6) intercepted by an AuthInterceptor that shares the EXACT SAME RefreshGuard/session AxiamClient's REST transport uses — never a second guard instance. public sealed class AxiamGrpcAuthzClient : IDisposable Inheritance object AxiamGrpcAuthzClient Implements IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The wire tenant_id/subject_id fields are resolved from the CURRENT access token's claims — preferring a signature-VERIFIED resolution via JwksVerifier (D-02) when available, falling back to an unverified decode purely as an operational hint (mirrors AxiamClient.DoHttpRefreshAsync's own established pattern) when no verifier is supplied or local verification fails — NEVER from the raw configured tenant identifier string. The real server (crates/axiam-api-grpc/src/services/authorization.rs) independently cross-validates both wire fields against its own verified JWT claims and rejects PERMISSION_DENIED on any mismatch, so a wrong hint here can only ever produce a denial — never an over-grant. Constructors AxiamGrpcAuthzClient(AxiamClient, Uri?) Constructs the gRPC authz transport from client's exposed internal seam (RefreshGuard, JwksVerifier, CurrentAccessToken, BaseUrl, CustomCaPem) — this constructor never modifies AxiamClient itself. public AxiamGrpcAuthzClient(AxiamClient client, Uri? grpcTarget = null) Parameters client AxiamClient The already-constructed REST AxiamClient whose session (RefreshGuard/tenant/token) this gRPC transport shares. grpcTarget Uri The gRPC endpoint. Defaults to client's own REST BaseUrl when omitted — pass an explicit value when AXIAM's AuthorizationService is exposed on a distinct host/port from the REST API. index.html.title: AXIAM .NET SDK | AXIAM .NET SDK
index.html.summary: AXIAM .NET SDK API reference for the AXIAM .NET packages: Axiam.Sdk — the client: cookie-session authentication (LoginAsync, VerifyMfaAsync, RefreshAsync, LogoutAsync), authorization (CheckAccessAsync, BatchCheckAsync), the gRPC authorization client, and the AMQP consumer with HMAC verification and replay protection. Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore — the resource-server middleware for ASP.NET Core: verifies the AXIAM JWT locally against a cached JWKS, enforces the cross-tenant claim check, and applies the CSRF double-submit check to cookie-authenticated state-changing requests. See the AXIAM SDK contract for the behaviour every AXIAM SDK guarantees.
